perturbation.original_text: FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds' investment objectives, investment policies, portfolio holdings, performance, etc. The content and format of such information shall be as FIIOC, in its sole discretion, shall choose. FIIOC may change the format and/or content of such informational reports, and the frequency with which such information is provided. For purposes of Section 4.2 of each of the Company's Participation Agreement(s) with the Funds, FIIOC represents that it is the designee of the Funds, and Company may therefore use the information provided by FIIOC without seeking additional permission from the Funds.
perturbation.changed_text: FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds' investment objectives, investment policies, portfolio holdings, performance, etc. The content and format of such information shall be as FIIOC, in its sole discretion, shall choose. However, FIIOC shall maintain a consistent format for all informational reports delivered to Company and shall provide a minimum of 30 days written notice prior to changing any content included in the informational reports. For purposes of Section 4.2 of each of the Company's Participation Agreement(s) with the Funds, FIIOC represents that it is the designee of the Funds, and Company may therefore use the information provided by FIIOC without seeking additional permission from the Funds.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction regarding FIIOC's discretion over information format and content. The original text allows for arbitrary changes at FIIOC's sole discretion. The modified text requires consistent formatting and 30 days' notice for content changes. This contradicts the initial statement granting FIIOC sole discretion, especially if the participation agreements requires specific information to be disclosed.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Information to be Provided to Company. FIIOC agrees to provide to Company, on a periodic basis, directly or through a designee, information about the Funds' investment objectives, investment policies, portfolio holdings, performance, etc. The content and format of such information shall be as FIIOC, in its sole discretion, shall choose. However, FIIOC shall maintain a consistent format for all informational reports delivered to Company and shall provide a minimum of 30 days written notice prior to changing any content included in the informational reports. For purposes of Section 4.2 of each of the Company's Participation Agreement(s) with the Funds, FIIOC represents that it is the designee of the Funds, and Company may therefore use the information provided by FIIOC without seeking additional permission from the Funds.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly grants FIIOC sole discretion over the content and format of information provided. The changed text imposes a requirement for consistent formatting and 30 days' notice for content changes, which directly contradicts the initial statement of FIIOC's sole discretion. This represents a strong contradiction as it restricts FIIOC's previously unfettered control, creating potential for disputes over compliance and enforcement. This example fulfills the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Company may use the information provided by FIIOC in communications to individuals, plan trustees, or others who have legal title or beneficial interest in the annuity or life insurance products issued by Company, or representatives of any of these parties, and to prospective purchasers of such products or beneficial interests thereunder. If such information is contained as part of larger pieces of sales literature, advertising, etc., such pieces shall be furnished for review to the Funds in accordance with the terms of the Company's Participation Agreements with the Funds. Nothing herein shall give the Company the right to expand upon, alter the appearance of, or otherwise alter the information provided by FIIOC. Company acknowledges that the information provided it by FIIOC may need to be supplemented with additional qualifying information, regulatory disclaimers, or other information before it may be conveyed to persons outside the Company.
perturbation.changed_text: Company may use the information provided by FIIOC in communications to individuals, plan trustees, or others who have legal title or beneficial interest in the annuity or life insurance products issued by Company, or representatives of any of these parties, and to prospective purchasers of such products or beneficial interests thereunder. If such information is contained as part of larger pieces of sales literature, advertising, etc., such pieces shall be furnished for review to the Funds in accordance with the terms of the Company's Participation Agreements with the Funds. The Company is permitted to reformat the information provided by FIIOC as needed to fit different communication mediums. Company acknowledges that the information provided it by FIIOC may need to be supplemented with additional qualifying information, regulatory disclaimers, or other information before it may be conveyed to persons outside the Company.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces an internal contradiction by stating the 'Company is permitted to reformat the information provided by FIIOC as needed to fit different communication mediums,' but removed the line 'Nothing herein shall give the Company the right to expand upon, alter the appearance of, or otherwise alter the information provided by FIIOC.' This conflicts with the original intent that the Company should not alter the information provided by FIIOC, leading to potential disputes over what constitutes acceptable reformatting.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Use of Information by Company. Company may use the information provided by FIIOC in communications to individuals, plan trustees, or others who have legal title or beneficial interest in the annuity or life insurance products issued by Company, or representatives of any of these parties, and to prospective purchasers of such products or beneficial interests thereunder. If such information is contained as part of larger pieces of sales literature, advertising, etc., such pieces shall be furnished for review to the Funds in accordance with the terms of the Company's Participation Agreements with the Funds. The Company is permitted to reformat the information provided by FIIOC as needed to fit different communication mediums. Company acknowledges that the information provided it by FIIOC may need to be supplemented with additional qualifying information, regulatory disclaimers, or other information before it may be conveyed to persons outside the Company.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the explicit prohibition against altering the appearance of the information provided by FIIOC while simultaneously permitting reformatting. This creates a direct contradiction as reformatting inherently alters the appearance, leading to uncertainty about the permissible extent of modification, which directly contradicts the original intent and creates ambiguity.
